Every time I call and request a supervisor, I am either: Told no supervisor is available. Told the supervisor only speaks French. Transferred to another regular employee pretending to be a supervisor. When I finally managed to speak to someone claiming to be a supervisor, they were incredibly rude and unprofessional — which sadly has been the norm with their staff throughout this process. It’s clear their customer service department has no genuine desire to help; they seem to deliberately stall cases until customers eventually give up. Adding to the dishonesty: Last year, after months of frustration, I left a detailed review on TripAdvisor and Facebook. Not long after posting, someone who seemed to be a Royal Air Maroc employee reached out, apologizing and expressing regret over how long it was taking to resolve my case. They asked me for my claim information and my contact details, which I immediately provided. However, they never followed up. A few weeks later, when I checked back, I noticed that my review had been removed — and attempts to contact that employee again were completely ignored. It became clear to me that this is part of a larger tactic they use: they pretend to show concern just to get negative reviews taken down, then ghost you afterward. Bottom line: If you are considering flying with Royal Air Maroc, think twice. If anything goes wrong — especially with baggage — you will be on your own, dealing with one of the worst customer service teams I’ve ever encountered. They have poor communication skills, little to no accountability, and it feels like a deliberate strategy to wear customers down until they quit fighting for a resolution. I refuse to give up — I will continue to follow up weekly until this case is resolved. But sadly, I have zero faith in Royal Air Maroc’s ability to treat their customers fairly or professionally.

Could not get a consistent answer from anyone 1. Flight Departure changed 4 times in the two weeks up to departure date 2.) They were supposed to correct a name error on my ticket. This was 4 nailes and 3 phone calls and email a copy of my passport. Correction was never made. 3.) At airport the departure time on my ticket, the departure time on the board and the flight status on the company web site departure time were all different. I called the company again and the said Gate was closing at 930. I rushed to get there at 920 and the gate had not even opened yet. 4.) Seems like everyone I dealt with made up a different set of rules and when I pointed out the inconsistencies some of the agents got impatient. 5.) In the end, Morocco is a beautiful country so don't cheat yourself out of a chance to visit. Just find a different way to get there then Royal air maroc

NEVER AGAIN. FLIGHT WAS DELAYED OVER 8 HOURS. I SPENT THE NIGHT IN THE AIRPORT BECAUSE THEY MADE US STILL "CHECK IN" ON TIME. 9PM FLIGHT LEFT AT 530AM WITH NO UPDATE AS TO WHY GIVEN. MISSED CONNECTION AND DIDN'T GET TO FINAL DESTINATION UNTIL AFTER 2AM LOCAL TIME. I HAVE CALLED NUMEROUS TIMES, WRITTEN EMAILS, FILLED OUT THEIR COMPLAINT FORM AND NO RESPONSE AT ALL. 3 OUT OF 4 OF OUR FLIGHTS WERE SEVERELY DELAYED WITH ABSOLUTELY NO EXPLANATION WHATSOEVER. AVOID AT ALL COSTS.

Disappointing Business Class Experience – Unacceptable Attitude We flew business class on April 15th, expecting a premium experience—but were shocked by the behavior of one of the air hostesses. She was extremely impolite, bordering on rude, and showed zero professionalism. When we asked for meals for our children, she flatly refused, claiming everything was finished. Upon insisting—given we had paid a significant amount for this service—her response was shockingly inappropriate: “I’ll bring my own food for your children.” That level of sarcasm and dismissiveness is completely unacceptable, especially in a service role. We understand things can run out, but there’s a way to handle it with basic courtesy and empathy. If she no longer enjoys her job, perhaps it’s time she considered a different one—because passengers shouldn’t be paying to deal with such attitude and tantrums. Extremely disappointing experience from what should have been a high-end, customer-focused service.

Trust the all the negative reviews … it’s true…it’s BAD BEYOND BELIEF! This review would be minus stars if possible. We did read the reviews before travelling and thought it can’t be that bad… well it was worse. We had low expectations for RAM but they far exceeded our worst nightmares. Upon booking through American Airlines (more on them later) we really did not have much of a choice for the return from Morocco back to the states as AA partners with RAM. That being said we were initially supposed to fly Casablanca to Miami but a couple of weeks before the trip RAM completely cancelled that leg so we now had to do Casablanca – JFK – MIA. We got to the airport over 3 hours before our flight as we thought there may be issues. We arrived at the check-in counter, gave our last names and passports and were met with an “hmmmmm” followed by “I don’t see your reservation” and “do you have a confirmation number”. Of course we did, told her and then “uh-oh, I still don’t see it, do you have a ticket number?”, obvious stall tactics. I fired up my computer and found the ticket number and then they said “American Airlines never confirmed your ticket”. Really as we have confirmation numbers, ticket numbers and am looking at my AA app as we speak seeing everything online booked and paid for. Then the truth came out “the flight is oversold and we can’t check you in”. They asked us to move away from the desk saying “we will put you on the waitlist” while they checked other people in (locals). We refused; we have a confirmed booking on this flight. While still in line we called AA and spoke to a nice lady (for 50 minutes) who confirmed that we did indeed have seats on this RAM flight but there no options to get us out of Casablanca that day or the next day. They were checking in dozens of other people as we were waiting and on hold with AA. As it turned out there were other guests (mostly flying American Airlines) that had the same issue that we did. Frantic…with my wife and 12-year-old daughter in tears there was no help from anyone at RAM, employees, supervisors and managers, no answers, no options, no help at all. I resorted to looking to book another flight at our own expense which would have flown us from Casablanca to Istanbul, 6-hour layover and then to JFK. Just before I bought these tickets my daughter comes running over to say they got us on the flight, I ran back to the desk to get us checked-in, now 50 minutes before the flight leaves. The agent proceeded to put 2 of our suitcases on the conveyer belt and as I load our third bag our agent gets up mid check-in and walks away! Two bags gone with no bag tracking number, no QR code…nothing, no boarding passes and additional bags still needed to check. Fast forward to arrival in Miami, missing checked bag and took a week to arrive back at our house, this took daily calls and emails. Another agent gave us our boarding passes, now we are 40 minutes before the flight leaves, we ran as fast as we could, had to go through security (had to cut the line) and then passport control, again cut the line. Ran to gate as doors were closing. Made the flight and was then told we were not sitting together, they wanted my 12-year-old daughter to sit on her own between strangers on a flight from Casablanca to New York, needless to say that was not happening. We would not take our seats; flight delayed until a nice gentleman offered to change seats so at least my wife and daughter could sit together, absolutely no help from the flight attendants. I was placed in the middle seat of 5 in the back of the plane, we had 3 seats booked together. They lie, they did have our reservation. They did not honor our seat assignments. They did not honor the checked bag policy for status members. Planes are terrible, old, hot, uncomfortable and food equally bad. Staff are downright rude. Both of us are in the hospitality/travel industry and travel a lot. Never in our lives have we encountered this level of incompetency and absolute disregard for customer needs. Insofar as American Airlines (partner with RAM), they said we booked with RAM so there was nothing they could do. We booked the R/T flights with AA, not directly with Royal Air Maroc. Thanks American Airlines, sure glad I flew over a million miles with you and when I really needed help you left us to fend for ourselves. PLEASE learn from our mistake, DO NOT fly this airline at any cost!

I am writing to express my frustration with the repeated mishandling of my luggage by Air Maroc. This is the third time my luggage has been misplaced. The first two instances occurred during my return trips home, but the most recent incident happened while I was traveling outbound. My bag contained essential clothing, and I was forced to purchase new clothes. The airport and the office at Monrovia have shown a lack of organization. There was no tracking system or tracking number provided for my luggage. It has been over two weeks, and I have yet to receive any follow-up from the airline.

I am writing to express my extreme disappointment and frustration regarding the recent experience my travel companions and I had with Royal Air Maroc. What was supposed to be a pleasant trip turned into an incredibly stressful and disappointing ordeal due to the airline’s mishandling of our baggage and subsequent lack of customer service. We were traveling from Errachidia (AT1447), with a connection in Casablanca (AT924), before arriving at our final destination in Seville, Spain. Upon arrival in Seville, we discovered that two of our large checked bags were missing. These bags contained not only essential clothing and toiletries but also valuable personal belongings and cherished souvenirs that were irreplaceable. We immediately filed a Property Irregularity Report (PIR) at the airport and expected to receive some form of follow-up or assistance. However, despite our repeated attempts to contact Royal Air Maroc, we received absolutely no response for an entire day. This complete lack of communication added to our distress and left us feeling utterly abandoned by the airline. As a result of the lost baggage and silence from your support team, we were forced to spend our entire stay in Seville without any of our personal items, clean clothes, or travel essentials. What was meant to be a memorable trip was overshadowed by inconvenience, discomfort, and unnecessary stress — all due to your airline’s negligence. This experience is unacceptable. We demand an immediate investigation into the whereabouts of our luggage, a formal apology, and full compensation for the inconvenience and loss we’ve suffered. We trusted Royal Air Maroc with our belongings, and that trust has been thoroughly broken. I sincerely hope this complaint will be taken seriously, and that action will be taken to resolve this matter promptly.

Worst customer service i have ever seen. I missed my flight because information on website was wrong. Wanted to fly with my bicycle and they wanted to charge me over 275$ cad for it, while its listed at 150cad$ on the website. It not possible to pay online and since my credit card was deactivated i missed my flight. Nobody tries to help you, they all stare at you with no sense of sympathy. Worst airline, worst customer service i have ever seen

My flight was scheduled for 9:00 PM. Got an email that it is delayed to 12:00 am. Get another email that it is delayed to 4:30 am. I have tour reservation in Casablanca and am going to miss it. They cannot tell me what is wrong.

I recently had a terrible experience with Royal Air Maroc. I had a 12-hour layover in Casablanca on my way home, and before my flight, the airline was contacted to inquire about hotel accommodations for long layovers. They assured that they provided such accommodations, for layovers of 8 hours and above, so I canceled my outside hotel booking based on this information. First of all, the state of the plane was deplorable. The plane was extremely dirty. The meal tray was filthy, and I had to use wet tissues to clean both my seat and the tray. When i arrived at the airport, I asked multiple airline officials about the promised hotel accommodation, but they were rude and claimed no such service was offered. Instead, they directed me to the Oasis Lounge, which was overcrowded and poorly maintained. There were not enough seats, people were standing, and I couldn’t even use the restroom without risking losing my seat. Passengers were even SLEEPING ON THE FLOOR. The airline provided no food, water, or comfort during this long layover. Despite my efforts to get assistance, including having my family reach out to customer care via phone and email, no one was helpful or responsive. It was a terrible experience from start to finish. I would never fly with Royal Air Maroc again and strongly advise others to avoid this airline as well.

We transited arriving from a Turkish Airlines Istanbul business class flight, to a RAM flight via Laayoune to Gran Canaria. 1. Our luggage was mislaid for 48 hours at Casablanca Airport 2. The inflight staff of RAM flatly refused to speak English. no announcements were made in English 3. Further they never informed us that we were required to exit the flight at Laayoune and re board, and for that the head inflight attendant chose to scream at us For the future RAM should advise ALL ENGLISH speaking clients at the time of making a booking with their airline that they only offer services in their vernacular.
Flight was 8hours delayed without an early notice , bad customer service and the food made me feel sick .this is so frustrating

I am Moroccan, and I will never book with this airline again in my life. This airline is an embarrassment to our country. High prices for absolutely abhorrent customer service, garbage policies, and rude airport staff. I love my country but we our customer service is T-R-A-S-H and Royal Air Maroc is at the bottom of the barrel. If you call with any issue, big or small, they are not helpful. They are rude, dismissive, and I think the entire world should boycott them. That's the only way they'll ever improve. Royal Air Maroc gives Morocco and Moroccans a bad reputation, and this is coming from someone that is Moroccan. Avoid at all costs, unless you want a headache and horrible flying experience. Their planes are often not properly cleaned, with a weird smell as well. Don't waste your money on business class, because the business class experience on Royal Air Maroc is nothing like business class with other airlines..you might as well fly economy. Food is gross, air staff is rude, customer service phone staff is a nightmare, and even the people on the ground at the airport are rude. It's like being rude and dismissive and lazy are qualities required to work for this airline. Absolutely disgusting and shameful. They will never see another penny from me or my large extended family of 14 people who fly from Canada/US to Morocco several times a year. Shame on them! Take it from a Moroccan...please spend a little extra and fly with another airline, I promise you it's worth it. They don't deserve a penny from anyone. Garbage airline, garbage service. Scammers, money grubbers, horrible.

I booked a more expensive ticket because I needed the option of changing it. I had some uncertainties with work and needed the extra flexibility. Unfortunately, when I called to change my return flight, the airline asked for an extra $334. They said they were going to email me a link to pay this extra fee but the link never arrived. I called back asking for the link to be resent and their said they cancelled the request and I now have no return flight. They then did the whole thing again but now to change would cost $447. I said I wanted to talk to a manager and they hung up the call. I called back again and again and each time I asked to talk to a manager the call dropped. Finally, I got through but now they said that because it's Ramadan I need to call back after the holiday. I did that, and now the price to change was $580. Each of the 11 times I called I landed on a customer service agent that was dismissive, rude, short, and kept interrupting me to blame me for their system's failure. An absolute abhorrent experience. Would never book with this airline again.

Easily the worst, most disorganized, outdated, poorly run airline I’ve ever been on. Read the reviews-they’re accurate-it truely is a terrible experience. From the chaos of check in, to seats that we paid for and never received, to steaming hot bus rides to board the plane, horrendous food, poor service, bathrooms that are locked until cruising altitude, dirty cabin and seats, surely flight attendants, it goes on and on. AVOID this airline at all cost!!

Worst Flight Ever. They cancelled the flight anytime. Do not take this flight ever, they don't respond in timely manner as well. For my refund, I'm trying to reach out to them from last 20 days, they keep delaying and don't give nay accurate answer.

WOW food service on a short flight. Unheard of. This was a surprise. Sorry we ate at the airport. They should have informed us. No inflight entertainment. Gotta lets us know this stuff too . Bathroom was dirty as usual for any airline. (does any airline have clean bathrooms ??) let me know - Only issue we had was flight was changed to an hour earlier which threw off our sightseeing since we were in Casablanca for just 1 night. OH well no big deal it was raining anyway. Just please please airlines stop all the over head luggage. It's driving me nuts watching people fight for space which is not theirs to even take. It should cost more to take carry on over head luggage than to put it under the place. This must be unsafe. Not sure why this airline gets a bad rap. If we had known how good it was for the price we would have flown internally in Morocco and stayed a lot longer. NEXT TIME FOR SURE!

We had 3 flights, 2 from Toronto and one return flight. One the first flight, they lost our luggage for 24 hours. On the next flght, they were delayed 10 hours. On the final flight, they were delayed 24 hours. RAM deserves the terrible rating.

The flight from Lagos to London via Casablanca was the worst flight I've ever been on. The flight was delayed for 80 minutes with no explanation from the captain or officials. The air hostesses were rude while boarding and during the flight. The aircraft was rickety and reeks of foul smell. Will not recommend and will definitely not be flying agin with them.

We flew from São Paulo (Brazil) to Barcelona (Spain) with a layover in Casablanca (Moroco). The intercontinental flight was very good (modern plane, good food with 2 full meals). The flight from Casablanca to Barcelona was also more than fine (comfortable seat and snack on the way). Despite lot of negative reviews we decided to go with them and were very pleased with the experience

The Worst Airline Experience of My Life – Avoid at All Costs I have flown with many airlines, but this was, without a doubt, the absolute worst travel experience I have ever had. If I could give them negative stars, I would. This airline should not be in business. They are disorganized, incompetent, and utterly incapable of providing even the most basic level of customer service. I paid $2,500 for a business class ticket, expecting at least a decent long-haul experience. What I got instead was a dirty, cramped, and uncomfortable business class seat in a 2-2-2 configuration, where I had to share foot space with another passenger. To make things worse, one of the seats was uneven when laid flat, meaning sleeping was nearly impossible. How is this considered business class? But the real disaster happened during my layover in Casablanca. We had a tight connection, and upon arrival, there was no clear signage, no information on departure times, and absolutely no communication from the airline. We asked multiple airport staff, including the help desk, security officers, and even the police, and every single one of them told us the wrong information. They claimed our flight had already left an hour earlier, forcing us to panic and attempt to rebook. In the midst of this chaos, a random non English speaking stranger (more helpful than the entire airport staff) pointed us in the right direction. We sprinted to the correct gate and barely made the flight. If we had followed what the airport employees told us, we would have been stranded with no options. That level of incompetence is unacceptable. For the return flight, the nightmare continued. Our gate changed four times within the final hour and a half before departure, creating unnecessary confusion and stress. When we finally landed in JFK, they lost my checked baggage. No apologies, no help, just more frustration. This airline is an absolute joke. Disorganized, misleading, and completely unreliable. I wouldn't fly with them again if they paid me. If you're considering booking a flight, save yourself the headache and choose literally any other airline

I had an absolutely terrible experience flying with Royal Air Maroc, and I would strongly advise against ever booking with them. From missed connections to unjustified denied boarding, their handling of my journey was beyond unacceptable. 1. Denied Boarding for No Reason (15/03) I arrived on time at Casablanca Airport for my flight to London (AT806 at 13:45), made it to the gate 10 minutes before closure, and was still denied boarding. The reason? I was “not on the list.” Meanwhile, a male passenger on the exact same itinerary was allowed to board. When I asked for an explanation, none was given. Even worse, before my first flight that day was even delayed, I had already received an email saying I had been moved to the next day’s flight—without my consent. When I called customer service, they reassured me that if I made my original connection, I could board as scheduled. That was a lie. This resulted in a completely unnecessary 24-hour delay to my trip. 2. Delayed Flight & Missed Connection (07/03) On 07/03, my flight from London to Casablanca (AT801) was delayed, causing me to miss my connection to Dakhla. I was rebooked onto a flight the next morning, meaning I arrived at my destination over 9 hours late. No proper apology, no real assistance—just another headache to deal with. 3. Unsafe & Unacceptable Accommodation After missing my connection, I was placed in a run-down hotel near Casablanca Airport. As a young woman traveling alone, I felt extremely unsafe staying there. No proper security, no basic amenities, and absolutely no consideration for my comfort. To make matters worse, no food vouchers were provided, so I had to cover my own meal expenses. When I was denied boarding on 15/03, they offered me the same terrible hotel. At that point, I refused and had to pay out of pocket for a safer place to stay. Conclusion: Avoid This Airline at All Costs Royal Air Maroc showed zero care for their passengers and provided some of the worst customer service I’ve ever experienced. Their incompetence caused me multiple delays, extra expenses, and unnecessary stress. I would NEVER recommend them to anyone.

OMG! Absolutely the WORST airline ever! We have been sitting on the runway for nearly two hours now with ZERO information on why we have not taken off. Told earlier is was due to weather but planes are taking off and landing right before our eyes. The staff will not allow anyone to use the toilet, and are yelling at passengers to stay in there seats. Never Again! Do yourseld and your family a big favor and fly another airline. This one SUCKS!

The worst service I have ever experienced. Flight delays and cancellations of more than 24 hours without any compensation or apology from the airline. Customer support is practically useless and solves nothing. During the booking process, I made a mistake by swapping the passenger's first and last name, and I was told to buy new tickets. I have never encountered such unprofessional behavior from staff and, frankly, such disregard for customers.

In over 30 years of global travel, I have never encountered an airline as disgraceful, unaccountable, and discriminatory as Royal Air Maroc (RAM). While Morocco is a beautiful country with a rich culture, RAM’s systematic mistreatment of passengers especially Black people and Africans tarnishes its reputation. I booked a round-trip ticket from Paris to Cotonou directly through RAM. My NYC to Paris flight was smooth, and with a 12-hour layover, I stepped out for errands. Upon returning, I received an SMS delay notification, pushing my flight from 18:30 to 19:10 (though it didn’t actually depart until 20:00). Despite arriving at 16:20 with only 1 carry-on, I was denied boarding with reason that I was late. I had a family emergency in Cotonou, and RAM’s reckless treatment forced me to buy a last-minute ticket through RwandAir to reach benin. When I tried to use my original return ticket home, RAM refused to honor it, forcing me to purchase another ticket at my own expense while the entire plane was barely half full. What followed in Casablanca was outright humiliation. The staff was hostile, dismissive, and openly discriminatory. A supervisor mocked my frustration and threatened to ban me for insisting on my rights. Another boldly stated that Morocco “doesn’t care if Africans fly through the country” and would be “better off without Africans in particular.” This level of blatant racism and disrespect is unacceptable. This isn’t an isolated incident RAM has a Skytrax rating of 3/10, with endless complaints about: Unexplained flight delays and cancellations. Passengers denied boarding despite valid tickets. Rude, unprofessional, and openly discriminatory staff. Consistent humiliation of Black travelers, particularly Africans. A lack of accountability in refunding or honoring paid tickets. Royal Air Maroc thrives on exploitation, racial bias, and utter disregard for passenger rights. Such behavior would never be tolerated in the U.S. The fact that RAM continues to operate this way without consequences is shameful. Morocco deserves better. The world deserves better. Black travelers deserve better.

Avoid this airline if you value your time and comfort. Boarding was an absolute mess—no organization, no clear announcements, just a chaotic free-for-all. Once onboard, the service was just as bad: indifferent crew, slow responses, and barely any amenities. To make matters worse, the airline is stuck in the past—no digital check-in, no in-flight WiFi, and outdated systems that make everything painfully inefficient. Definitely not flying with them again.
I tried to print my boarding passes online only to find out that because im flying from Canada I have to go to the ticket counter. ridiculous.
The worst airline in every category. Our luggage got lost. Non of the monitors worked. Food was disgusting. Not a friendly personals.

This is by far the worst airline with the worst customer service I have ever experienced. They lost my hand luggage, which I was forced to check in due to lack of space, and I never got it back. Despite multiple attempts to contact them, they do not respond or take any responsibility. To this day, I have not received any reimbursement or even a proper acknowledgment of my issue. The complete lack of professionalism and accountability is shocking. I would never recommend anyone to fly with this airline! Avoid at all costs!

I bought a ticket, and the next day, scammers with a phone number from Morocco started to chat with me in Whats App (as a woman who wants to be friends) and ask about my personal data. It's so fast; it seems like they have some automatic process to give/sell phone numbers to scammers. (I had nothing in common with Morocco; I never was there and used the mobile number attached to Whats App the first time in a year to buy the ticket)

I stayed on the airport for 2 nights without them offering me a good contort able place to sleep. They did not even care about our safety no food.the well of my suitcase was broken. I have lot to say but I will stop here. It is have been more than 6 months now I made a complaint about my last trip with Moroco Arlines and nothing is been done up to today. I have been told to receive a compensation refund. I sent all the documents that I have been asked to send. Which I did but I did not received any compensation refund up to today . I am really disappointed 😔 with your services

Royal Air Maroc is pretty bad. They lost my bag, and said it was their responsibility. Most of the hostesses, they are irresponsible and do not care about serving Royal Air Maroc customers. Never ever book with this ridiculous airline company.

I learned to fear RAM over the years, I don't travel that much, but when I do they manage to loose my luggage. Today, they just dis it again. No one there at the airport to support us, people with missing luggage, when you talk to them they don't know anything and it clearly feels they don't care. Worst company ever, avoid it at all costs, and if you can't, pray with your fingers crossed.

It’s by far one of the worst airline from delay prospective, I have been flying with them for many years, they never flew on time, but I have to use it as it’s the quickest one for my destination. Expect delays at any time of the year

After reserving a flight in Oct of 2024, I was told the day before my flight that I need to pay 292 GBP more (on a 454 GBP flight ticket) in order to "confirm my seat" and be removed from standby. This was a confirmed ticket and was not changed. We were told that "iberia" does not provide the class of service we purchased and needed to upgrade this class on RMA to ensure our flight on Feb 26. They offered a free change to Feb 28th!!!! WTH!

Horrible horrible horrible - HORRIBLE!!!!!! You have to climb and walk down stairs and I had Carey on luggage and the woman with strollers had no assistance. 2 people fell down the stairs. Then the condition of the aircraft was so dirty and old. They put a rose in the bathroom to try and what make it seem upscale. The food presentations was decent but the taste was dry bland and flavorless. The amount paid for a business class ticket it was more like flying in premium economy- the plane smells like old urine. The only thing that got a one star was the staff. They were great! By the way going was a different experience it was better.

We just returned from our trip to Morocco. I can say without hesitation that Royal Air Maroc is the worst airline I have ever travelled on, and I've been on some pretty bad ones. First, the planes are absolutely filthy. As we boarded, we saw food remnants from previous flights on the floor and on the seats. There was also some sort of putrid gummy substance stuck on the seat in front of me that got onto my clean jeans. I had to ask our first hotel to help me clean my pants (no amount of soap and water worked). They had to put my pants in a freezer to get this vile, stinking, gummy mess to come off. Then there is the repulsive food on Royal Air Maroc - which is totally inedible - the worst we have ever been served on an airline. One bite of the "beef" entree and I thought I would throw up. We refused all food on our flight back home. The Royal Air Maroc "entertainment system" is so old and unreliable that it constantly shut off on our 8-hour flight home. I had to restart the movies/shows at least 30 times (no exaggeration) on our flight. Add to the above, the endless delays, the truly stupid 1 hour wait in Royal Air Maroc's pre-board line before the official pre-board line (we had already been through 3 security checks), and the filthy, disgusting bathrooms. I will NEVER travel on Royal Air Maroc again.

My worst experience ever: Flight was cancelled due to technical problems with the airplane - all passengers were kept at the gate for 7 (Seven!) hours, last two hours without water and food. No information was provided… The worst airline ever - try to fly with someone else!

I would not fly this airline if my life depended on it. The boarding process to get on board this flight is akin to a hellscape. They made no announcements and you had to navigate 2 layers of security checks with lines that were unmanaged. Once you got past the first checkpoint there were no chairs and since I was traveling with my mother who can not stand that long, I waited back. Luckily, I encouraged us to get in line before the very end because they overbooked the flight by ~20 individuals and started turning away people at the gate. When we finally got to Washington, the airtag on my bag was still showing Casablanca. They made me wait for every single bag to get off the carousel before letting me lodge a complaint (~90 minutes extra wait). Then when I was about to leave the airport without my bag, they called me back saying my bag was there. It was NOT, instead there was a random bag that they had tagged to my name. It then took them 4 days to ship my bag to Washington and another 8 days for them to return the bag to me in Pittsburgh where I live. Thank god for my airtag because it took incessant follow up and tracking my bag to get them to do anything. Plus, they did not even deliver the bag to me. They required that I drive 40 minutes to the Pittsburgh airport to collect my bag. When I finally got it, the bag was beat up and ripped. Because it had been more than 5 days since my flight, I was owed compensation. I had filed a complaint on January 9th (5 days after my flight) and it took them until Febuary 14th to respond. When they responded, they said it had past the 21 day window and there was nothing they could do to help. I am still owed compensation by the airline for their mistake and I doubt I ever will. I would never fly this airline again and highly encourage you not to subject yourself to it.

I have never encountered such a disgusting flight in my life. The staffs are old and terrible they are so mean and very very disrespectful. They are not clean. One of them was stinking so much. Disgusting how they are flight attendants without maintaining hygiene. The food was extremely terrible and when asked for sugar they refused to give more sugar. No passenger should be treated this way. Zero respect and the attitude is disgusting. Zero recommendations for this flight.

🚨 WARNING 🚨 DO NOT fly with Royal Air Maroc! My experience—and thousands of others’—was a nightmare. They lost two of our bags on the way to Morocco and another on our return to Miami, which was missing for two months. Their excuse? No compensation because they eventually delivered the bags—after two months. Customer service is nonexistent. No direct communication, and they take 15 to 20 days to respond to emails if they reply. Avoid this airline at all costs!

Having found it impossible to get through to RAM via phone (over 45 mins holding only to be hung up on) or online, I made my way to the airline NYC office in Rockefeller Center. There, the combined efforts of Nathan and Fatima exceeded any expectation. Stellar and creative customer service solved the issue. If all the staff at RAM are as professional, competent and client oriented as Nathan and Fatima, RAM is in good hands. 0/10 for phone service while 11/10 for in person service.

They have an absolutely disregard for passengers! I had a transit ticket Toronto - Casablanca - Barcelona. At Casablanca airport, I waited 5 hours for the next flight. My flight was at 4 pm to Barcelona, but they did not even announce boarding! When I went to inquire at 3:40 pm, they had already closed boarding and there was no one at the check-in counter. Not only did I not board the plane, but other people too. They did not even bother to announce over the microphone the passengers who did not show up for boarding! I travel a lot, but I have never seen such a terrible attitude in any airline. I would like to say separately about Casablanca airport. It was impossible to find people there to resolve the issue of changing the flight. There was no one at the counters. There was only a telephone that you had to call and report the problem! I had a hard time getting through. They made me buy a separate ticket for the Casablanca - Barcelona flight. I had to sit on a chair for a long time and the night. The hotel, which was in the transit area, was closed, although the information desk showed me where it was, they did not even know that it was closed. They answered reluctantly, no respect for passengers. Those who do not have a Moroccan visa are not allowed out of the transit area. It was a terrible experience! I do not recommend anyone to go through this with this terrible airline and such a terrible airport!

Poor service, unprofessional and indifferent. In top of that I’ve never received my luggage. They sent an email saying that I will get refunded for that, it’s been four months no refund received yet. Fatima the Ticketing manager didn’t even bother picking the phone and let her agent dealing with the issue.

Absolute crap of an airline. Hard to believe that they still exist and have business. Overbooked my 12hr international direct flight, and said they could only replace with a second day transfer flight. Just a total joke and disgrace of an airline. Never fly this!!!

I have travelled from Toronto to Malaga Spain through Casablanca just after Christmas. My Luggage did not arrive with me and i was without my clothing for 4 days. Instead of enjoying my vacation i needed to shop. On the way back the same thing happened. My suitcase has arrived after 5 days. This is very bad service. If I ever travel with this airline, it will be only with my backpack. The experience was not fun at all. Wasted time and money. I have filed a complain.......nothing. I traveled from London to Abidjan via Casablanca on the 14th of January and I have still not received my luggage from Royal air Maroc two weeks now from my travel date . Although a property irregularity report was filed automatically once arrived at destination nothing has been done until now . I find myself in a terrible situation.I have contacted Royal air Maroc by phone even walk in their agency in Abidjan 3 times but nothing. I am able to track my luggage so I shared the location with them but the luggage is still at the same position for 2 weeks .This is an appalling situation for an international airline to provide such a bad service and a shame to carry such a poor reputation by not caring about passengers belonging. I am still waiting for this situation to get fixed !

Terrible customer service and satisfaction with them is 0 . I am still waiting for a miserly refund on lost baggage since July 24 . Do not travel with Air Maroc !!! When something goes wrong they are not going to help satisfactorily. Never again will I fly with them

Doha-Casablanca-Gambia on 24th Jan. 25. QR could not issue Boarding Passes though having issued ticket. Eventually, I got BPs printed out quickly while boarding in Doha. Embarked after being bussed around in Doha airport. Aircraft was a newer Dreamliner B787-900, filled to last seat. Was in the middle of a midsection in the back, surrounded by passengers all over. However, the seats are spacious and can recline 20 cm. I felt comfortable and slept quite well. Food was served on large trays and main course was very delicious (spicy seafood) with plenty of support like heavy salad, desert, cheese and chocolate. I could choose my favourite meal despite being on of the last being served. Got an extra portion on request. On this 9-hour night flight the two meal rounds were valued and sufficed. Drinks plenty, including Moroccan wines and beers. Staff, 90% are Moroccan males, are professional, hardworking, focused, and pragmatic. They may appear being blunt but on firm requests they act well. They relocate seats up to last Minute and beyond to accommodate passengers wishes, allowing couples and families to sit together. Amenity kit, hear-sets, pillows, blankets provided to all. IFA promised a lot with big screens and options. However, it was not working properly for approx. half of the cabin, it froze again and again or ran into endless loops. In Casablanca, RAM kept their announced promises of providing a smooth STPC: hotel, meals and transport for long-hours transit passenger. Passed immigration without hurdles. I was put up in the fine 3-4 star Zenith hotel and got two meals within a 12 hours stay. Back to airport, accessed the RAMs Zenith Lounge which is spacious, fine: sufficient catering and tarmac views in two floors. Upper floor has a manned bar, while the lower have barista and meal catering. Lively and exotic, a surprisingly good lounge, including local wines of fair quality. Last leg, Casablanca to Banjul on a B737-800 was also a pleasure. Wide spaced seats, I was given my preselected exit seat without charges. Good, hot meal provided with three options. FAs were professional, all males. Catered well for, appreciated their seafood and wines. Recall, this is only a 3 hours’ flight with an impressive fully serviced catering with drink-rounds. Generous airline providing at least 2 x 23 kg luggage allowance for everyone Luggage’s arrived quickly and in good condition at final destination. Only minor spots: IFE unreliability and bus-gating in DOH and CMN. These are easily outweighed by other perks the airline provides. Ticket not expensive. First time with RAM, pleasantly surprised, can’t really fault them; fully recommended.
Arrived to my destination, but not receiving my luggage is This is my worsted hairline ever And I haven't received the luggage yet I’m still waiting for it

If you are thinking to purchase Royal Air Maroc (RAM) tickets please reconsider. Basically they were not responsive and negligent. We had three days before the cruise in Amsterdam that we wasted trying to get answers to: if they found the two bags or they had been lost. The answer was always “we are tracing your bags” uncommitted. We had incurred some expenses purchasing some clothes, necessities, etc. for upcoming trip considering that the baggage was lost or stolen. I tried to file a complaint on the RAM app on line but the app was malfunctioning and wouldn’t accept the information at all. I was able to do it at the later day but they responded that their regulations require filing a complaint within 21 day from the flight day. The airplane was quite old and not properly cleaned. The food and service were bad. So it comes to the point if you want to have all the troubles and aggravations experience with such a company like RAM for a few dollars less?

Avoid Morocco Air – A Nightmare Experience Travelers, beware! Morocco Air’s poor business practices can leave you stranded and frustrated. They routinely overbook flights, you can have a you can have a confirmed seat and they’ll just dump you and if you’re bumped, they’ll rebook you only if you pay an outrageous same-day “upcharge.” Don’t expect compensation or any meaningful assistance—they simply don’t prioritize their customers. If you must fly with Morocco Air, here are some crucial tips: • Invest in Travel Insurance: Ensure you have a comprehensive policy to cover the inevitable disruptions. • Use a Reliable Credit Card: Choose one with charge dispute options to protect yourself financially. • Allow Extra Time: Plan for delays by scheduling your trip with a buffer of 3–4 days. Unfortunately, Morocco Air’s lack of professionalism and ethics is an everyday occurrence. Spare yourself the stress and consider alternative airlines whenever possible. By the way, five people on my flight were in the same boat with me flying back to the United States. All confirmed paid passengers that they bumped with no way to get them home.

Flew Milan to Nouakchott, connecting in Casablanca. Booked directly with Air Maroc. Expensive economy ticket - this is not a budget airline. Fairly rudimentary website and app: special meals can only be requested by phone, not online; no chat. On the app, the bag tracking function doesn't work. Online check-in 48 hours in advance. Bag dropoff at MXP fairly efficient. Slow, chaotic boarding for both flights. Old aircraft without USB ports in economy class. Average seat comfort and meals. Both flights were late. For the first flight, at least they made an announcement and blamed "air traffic congestion". For the second flight CMN-NKC no announcements were made. The airport monitors showed the flight as "boarding" when the aircraft wasn't even at the gate! Boarding eventually started around the scheduled departure time and it was extremely chaotic, as the gate monitor showed the subsequent flight out of the same gate, and pax were lining up for the wrong flight. Once on board, the staff manually counted and recounted pax several times, then started checking baggage receipts for every pax on board, a highly unusual procedure. It became obvious that there was a problem with a passenger's luggage, as the staff continued to argue with them. Eventually the flight departed 1 hour and 35 min late. Not a single announcement was made and not a word of apology was offered. Would avoid this airline if possible.

Many years ago there was a game people played where the name of an airline was re-imagine as an unflattering acronym. For instance, name of the Belgian airline (at the time) Sabena was said to stand for "Such A Bad Experience - Never Again". I was reminded of that when I flew with Royal Air Maroc (RAM) from London, via Casablanca, to Marrakech in June 2024 - with the thought that the airline should be renamed "SABENA". I wasn't particularly planning to write a review, until I happened to look at the reviews for RAM on Tripadvisor, and finding - to my surprise - that many people had had a similar experience, with a missed connection in Casablanca and all that followed (including promised compensation that never materialised). My story will be familiar to many who have also flown on RAM in recent times. I shoud mention first, as some others have similarly done, that I used to fly to Morocco frequently some 40 and more years ago - always with RAM. They were a good airline then, by the standards of the day. However, the Royal Air Maroc of those times is unrecognisable in the outfit that calls itself Royal Air Maroc today. In June this year, then, I was flying to Marrakech from London and it had to be via Casablanca, if it was with RAM. The flight from London had ongoing delays, most of them for no visible reason. Even the RAM ground staff in the departure hall had not idea why it was taking so very long before boarding was allowed. In the end, the plane left more than two hours late. It arrived in Casablanca some time around 22:30 (or later), and took ages to cruise on the tarmace to a parking position miles from the terminal. The steps had to be brought to the plane, and coaches filled up before they go could drive to the terminal. In the end, the plane was so late and everything after landing took so long, that the connecting flight to Marrakech, with a departure time of 22:40, had long left. That was just the beginning. These things can happen, and a good airline will deal with them in a way that minimises the discomfort to passengers. In this case, no RAM staff member on the plane (and I asked for advice) was able to give any help at all. I was told to "ask someone on the ground" but of course there was no one around who would help. Thinking they might have held the connecting flight back, I ran through long corridors and up and down stairs to the domestic departure terminal, only to find it almost empty, with the last domestic flight of the day having left. I then had to go through the painful experience of going through immigration control (it was now almost midnight) and again no one, and certainly not anyone from RAM, was around to help. I was going to sleep on a bench in the terminal, until at around 01:00 I took a taxi to a hotel about 3 km from the airport, returning early the next morning to wait for the new connecting flight. Returning home a week later, I contacted Royal Air Maroc's customer service department, and was told to write in with the details (flight numbers, ticket numbers, cost of the airport hotel, and description of the general inconvenience). I had a reply fairly quickly offering me a voucher for €500 or a refund of €400. I chose the latter. I was asked to give them my bank account details, which was strange, as they could have paid the refund to the credit/debit card with which I had made the booking. Nothing happened. After a reasonable time, I called customer services. They confirmed they could see the correspondence online and that I was due this payment, and they assured me that it would come if I waited. This conversation was repeated a number of times over the following months. Each time the person in customers ervices was friendly and polite. They would promise to send a message right away to the finance office, and were certain that this message would do the trick. After about five months, though, it dawned on me, as it should have done earlier, that RAM were not going to make any payment, at any point. Reading the reviews of others recently confirmed what I had suspected - that RAM do not pay the compensation that they promise (or at the very least, there are a large number of people who have been promised compensation by RAM, which never arrived). I would advise against using RAM. If you really have to use RAM, though, then avoid at all costs having a connecting flight with RAM - whether through Casablanca or anywhere else.

A delayed flight was just the beginning of the chaos. When I arrived at JFK, my luggage was missing—and that was three months ago. Since then, I've received zero updates, zero accountability, and zero compensation. Every time I call customer service, I’m met with vague responses or outright indifference. It's like my missing luggage doesn’t matter to them at all. If I could undo my decision to fly with Royal Air Maroc, I would in a heartbeat. This experience has been a disaster, and I wouldn’t recommend this airline to anyone. Losing someone’s belongings is bad enough, but refusing to help or even communicate is unacceptable.

Terrible airline, poor and rude customer service, asking for refund with questions of deductions and keep getting the same reply everyday with no explanation then later got emails on each occasion to click on a link to confirm my agreement to the refund which is unusual. Avoid at all costs. I would never advise anyone to use this airline. The worst l have seen over 30 years of travelling.

Avoid like the plague!!! Flown with them a lot in the past as my wife’s parents are from Morocco. This means that unfortunately there is basically no other option than to take this joke of an airline. Bad service starts at check-in where they make us re-pack carry-on bags because of a few kg difference. Even if we pay more than a regular return ticket to the US would cost, we generally feel treated like we are flying Transavia. On the flight, the crew is some of the most uninterested crew I have ever experienced, and this is on multiple flights. The food is alright at best, and I guess the highlight of every flight. Experience at the Moroccan airport(s) is of course not much better, but I guess RAM doesn’t have much control over that (or do they…)

I am left shocked at how an airline can lose a customers luggage. You arrive at your destination bug your luggage is nowhere to be seen! HOW IS THAT EVEN POSSIBLE !!! This airline is a nightmare. I will never recommend to my enemy

WORST AIRLINE COMPANY: If anyone is thinking using Royal Air Maroc to flight ✈️ to West African countries. I came on trip of 5 days to Guinea going to Siguiri for next day morning 🇬🇳 and I arrived my luggage did not arrive. I will advise anyone in future not use this airline.
This is the worst airline , avoid this airline for your mental health, everything is abysmal. I beg you please and please DONT AND DONT. thank you

The major issue I had is I could not get movies to watch. Tje flight was approx 8 hours so o did want something to distract me. I called for the attendant 4-5 times and when the food was served, made a point of telling the steward. They did reset the program but it didn’t help. Fortunately, I was able to move to an empty seat behind md. That should not have happened though and I’m annoyed that my request was not attended to.

Worst airline I’ve used. Every flight was delayed. Missed all connecting flights- the second of which there were no other flights to our destination for 3 days. If you are flying with RAM just assume your departure is delayed by at least 1.5hrs.

Their flights are always delayed which makes it hard to schedule connections. They never give you any information but they are good at trying to hassle you. My brother have yet to be refunded for a ticket that was canceled due to covid nor did they offer him a credit. They never answer their emails. I wanted my receipt. I emailed them a year and a half ago, still no response. Avoid them at any cost.

I am beyond frustrated with Royal Air Maroc. After a previous terrible experience where they lost my luggage, I hesitantly gave them another chance, and now I regret it more than ever. They’ve done it again , lost my luggage. How is that even possible? The experience didn’t end there. When I contacted customer service, I specifically asked for my luggage to be delivered to my home in Tétouan. Instead, they informed me that it was sent to Tangier, and now I need to pick it up myself. Tétouan is far from Tangier, and this level of carelessness and lack of accommodation is simply unacceptable. Their call center staff were rude, dismissive, and completely unhelpful. They didn’t seem to care about resolving the issue or about the inconvenience they caused me. This is not how any professional airline should treat its customers. The overall service is abysmal. The prices are ridiculously high for what Royal Air Maroc offers, and the quality is nowhere near worth the cost. The staff, whether at the airport or on the phone, are unprofessional and untrained. I’ve experienced better service from budget airlines than from Royal Air Maroc, which is supposed to represent Morocco internationally!!!!!!!!!!!! This is the last time I will ever fly with Royal Air Maroc. Losing luggage twice, providing awful customer service, and showing no respect for their customers’ time or needs have convinced me that this airline is not worth the money, effort, or stress. If you’re considering flying with Royal Air Maroc, don’t. There are far better alternatives. Save yourself the headache and choose an airline that values its customers.hahaha even Ryanair is WAY BETTER than RAM. I hope this review serves as a warning to others and as a wake-up call for Royal Air Maroc to improve their service. Unfortunately, for me, it’s too late.

My flight from Montreal to Casablanca was okay. We left on time, service was okay and we arrived on time to Casablanca. I had a layover of 12h Casablanca and then took the next flight to Abidjan. We arrived on time, which is good… Now is the time to pick up our luggage and…NOTHING. Almost 65%-70% of the passengers did not receive their baggage. Is it normal for a company to flight their passenger without their luggage. Right now, I am in Abidjan with nothing to wear, absolutely nothing. We tried to contact the company, nothing. I never had good experience with RAM anyway. They lost one of my luggage in 2019 and never reimbursed me for it and another time, my luggage showed up totally unzipped. I’m pretty sure they don’t care but at least you know what to do.

Worst airline of all time. Do not even bother!. So we get to the airport in Casablanca and then some of us leave the airport because we have a 12 hour layover we come back and they tell us that our bag is supposed to add up to 10 KG which was not advertised and it was not a problem in Torontoso everybody here has to check and pay for an extra bag which was never discussed never so yeah you do not
Ils volent tout a Casablanca. Please be careful, they are thieves and you will lose all your luggages and you are warned.

Awful… they lost my bag again (third time this has happened). I have contacted them on numerous occasions and no response or answers . The second flight was also cancelled and so I did have to then fathom what to do at cassablanca airport at 1am with one baby. Did finally arrive at a hotel at 3am … but this is a small point to the overall awful customer service of the lost luggage.

The worse airline ever. I travelled from London Heathrow to Nigeria last year December 2023. It’s Dec 2024, I have still not received my luggage or any form of compensation. They promise to compensate me and up till now nothing yet. My baggage was targeted as it contained all of my jewelleries, wedding items, brand new gifts and valuables were in that particular bag. You guys are thieves, you have thieves as staffs. I have still not gotten over the trauma arriving Nigeria without my luggage. You guys cost me a lot and it affected me psychologically. Air Maroc pay me back my money .

You canceled my trip, wich i booked months in advance, a week before the planned trip and you made the transfer time so short (20 MINUTES!)that i would not have been possible to transfer from one plane to another.The flight cost € 599,-when i booked it.Your airline Royal Air maroc,-WHICH I ´LL NEVER FLY AGAIN-has ruined this planned trip to my home country.Yesterday i had buy a new ticket-by thAir France because of you the ticket cost twice as much as the one i originally bought.€1365.SHAMEONYOU

I travelled back from Bissau to Gatwick, first leg was fine but then I was double booked on the flight from Casablanca. There is only one flight per day and having not let me board Royal Air Maroc were terrible. Even now 8 weeks later I have had no response to my emails or been able to contact the airline.

Return flight to London,Gatwick from Casablanca was delayed by more than 8 hours and there was no communication from Air Morocco. After the incident, poor communication from the airline. Please avoid!!

I don't know why everyone is complaining about this airline. I travelled Fez to Cassablanca to Milan in September 2024. I could not fault them. They were better than Jetstar 100%. The check-in was easy, they were on time to leave, on time to the destination and my bags arrived. This is very rare with most airlines these days. The seats were comfortable and good leg room for me 165cm. Cassablanca to Milan had food service it was excellent. Arrived on time - other than bags taking longer than normal in Milan, I cannot fault this airline, I would fly with them again without hesitation and recommend them.

I am on day 5 of a 10 day trip without luggage. Air Maroc has been terrible. I flew from London to Yaounde, Cameroon via Casablanca on Wednesday 4th Dec. On arrival, it seems that nobody’s luggage had arrived and there had been in fact, a backlog of delayed luggage for quite some time. The luggage that had travelled on our plane actually belonged to passengers from earlier flights. There was no Air Maroc representative at the airport, only 2 airport workers who took over 2 hours to log all of the lost baggage reports. We were told our luggage would arrive on Saturday 7th December, and that we’d receive a phonecall to inform us. We had to chase it up yesterday, only to be told the luggage had not arrived and in fact they did not know where our luggage is. The Air Maroc World Tracer portal has been non-functional since it all started so we cannot even track our luggage. I will spare you the details of my personal difficulties in having travelled to a remote place without personal belongings. But I want to express my deepest anger and disgust at Air Maroc. A family member travelled from London to Ivory Coast 2 weeks ago with Air Maroc, and had exactly the same problem, only receiving their luggage 2 days before their trip ended. It is apparent that they have a HUGE backlog of delayed luggage, but are not informing passengers before flying. This is exceptional wickedness. The further insult to injury is the absolute lack of communication, which shows their lack of care, compassion and basic human respect for their customers. I will NEVER travel with Air Maroc again and strongly recommend that you do not either.

Horrible airline experience ever. Never recommend. Flight keep delaying for more than 3 hours. Lost baggage, extremely long queue and corrupted taking years to make the clame and taking weeks without getting luggage back. Don’t make the mistake to take it , you will regret !!!!

Worst airline ever. Don't recommend it. It has been 5 months I am waiting for the compensation since they cancelled my flight, and nothing has been done after following up more than 20 times. They don't act and don't follow European laws when flights are late or cancelled. Take Ryanair, it is definitely more premium service than RAM.

prior to flight I reserved a vegan meal. On the plane i was told that there is no booking for vegan meal at all. they did not offer anything, I had to ask at least for glass of juice. I had connecting flight with them which totalled me not eating for 30 hours. I can bear not eating. but humiliation i received from this company is unacceptable. Cabin crew member advised me to contact customer service to resolve the issue. i have contacted them after i got back home at the end of October. no reply, no contact since then, now it is December. I have called customer service in November, the reply from the lady was: i will send them an email. In short- horrible customer service.

As someone who has travelled the world over for decades, and travelled multiple airlines, I can confidently state that RAM is the worst I have encountered. It was like flying an airline stuck in the 1990s. Difficult to navigate website, employees who couldn't care less about anything, to lax onboard procedures..... they have it all! I understand that they are all government employees - and so difficult to fire, but come on! Would it take that much to smile once in a while!!! Most of them had the attitude that they didn't want to be at work. Check in at both JFK and CMN were dreadful. The planes were barely cleaned, and the flight staff thought they were doing the passengers a favor by providing service. On an 8 hr flight, there was only 1 attempt to collect trash. At no point did anyone actually come out and check seat belts and seat backs. On board, the entertainment options were not only limited, but extremely dated. The sound systems were dreadful. 2 out of every 3 charging ports didn't work. The food was practically inedible - cold, hard, and dry......and if warm, tasteless! This is 2024 folks! Even the worst budget airlines provide better service. Avoid this airline even if they are most convenient and cheap direct flight. Vacationing in Morocco is lovely. Don't let this dreadful and disgraceful national airline of Morocco ruin your vacation!

Okay your customer service is awful, my husband called and spoke to an agent at marrakesh airport and they were extremely rude, not at all helpful besides to say our luggage was sent to Brussels. He didn’t give him a number or offer to be of assistance, someone with your airline needs to step up and provide customer service to your customers. It has been 42 days without our luggage, you have shown lack of trying to help us and zero cares! We have filled out the required claim multiple time, sent you emails, sent you message on Facebook and instagram, this is ridiculous!

A total let down, arrived on the 30/11, baggage was left behind at Casablanca, I had a 3hr stopover, however plane was full so I guess too much baggage, next available flight from Casablanca with my baggage, 05/12. Useless.

had a transfer flight through Morocco with royal Moroccan airlines, made it on time to my connecting flight, they wouldn't let me board the plane but let other passangers board. clearly an overbooked flight and they simply told me to go to their comped hotel and catch the next days flight. i will not book royal Moroccan airlines again and i recommend no one else should either. all the best
Don't use this Airline - I wander how come they are still allowed to fly to europe. They misplaced by Luggage and they could not even be bothered to look for it -

The worst experience of my life. I'm handicapped and they refused to use the transfer chair they promised, made me walk, the flight attendant just wanted to be done, so he pulled me with great force, pulled me to the ground, broke my ankle and then refused to let me straighten it, pulling me the other way and breaking my fibula again. 3 fractures. They roughly grabbed and squeezed my broken ankle instead of the foot rest, causing more pain Then we get to the new gate and the transport employee tried to make me get up and walk on a broken ankle He just wanted the chair back. They insisted there were no doctors, we date for 3.5 hours in agony. Then they did the same, squeezing my broken leg while moving me on the plane. They refused to upgrade me to Business, even though they had empty seats. Then in Carthage the flight attendant clearly stated to his colleague that he hates doing transport and helping people. He ran as fast as he could down steep ramps. Then we get to baggage, they have lost my power chair, and demanded I walk to the car. Luckily we had some Embassy support to insist that this was absurd. We used their chair briefly to get to our car. The worst, most painful, traumatic experience of my life. I'm on 2 weeks bed rest, I'm missing my vacation with my grandchildren, I'm in intense pain, we have had to hire a nurse to help. I complained online, they ignored me, I complained on Facebook they ignored me They SHOULD upgrade me to Business for our return flights as 8 cannot sit in a chair for 3.5 hours and then another 7.5 hour flight I assume they will lose my power chair again This is the worst airline in the WORLD. If anyone knows how to get a response from them I would like to know

This is the worst airline on the planet. They made me pay them in cash to check bags. Moved my wife to America from Morocco. They overcharged me. Broke half of the items in the bags. Told me they didn’t accept credit card and they needed 6300 mad for the bags. That is a lie. They saw an American and saw dollar signs. They pocketed the money. My bags came in shambles. Thank you for ruining what should have been a great experience. Disgusting

I AM A MOROCCAN CITIZEN AND ALL I CAN SAY THIS IS THE WORST COMPANY THIS EARTH HAS PLEASE AVOID THIS CAUSE THEY WILL TREAT YOU LIKE A BEGGAR AND TRUST ME YOUR COMPLAINTS DOESN'T MATTER TO THEM AT ALL.

I was bumped off a flight when I had already checked in from then customer service was terrible. the only way to get back was to take another flight. i have emailed Royal Air Maroc and heard nothing yet

Our luggage was put on the wrong flight, AT636 instead of AT638. When we arrived back in the states we had no luggage. Delta was our connecting flight and has been helpful to find 2/3 bags. We are still missing one, finally after a month yesterday I received 1 message via Facebook from Royal Morocco Air prior to that nothing.
