I am writing this review on behalf of my father who just flew back last niggt after using RAM to go from DKR to Casa, and then Paris, back to Casa and New York, finally New York to Casa and Casa DKR. When he flew back to Casa from New York he was told by RAM staff to go to a specific gate for his connecting flight as his tickets did not mention anything. He sat there for 4 hours when someone finally came to him and told him he was at the wrong gate by then he missed his flight. The whole experience with this company was not pleasant by any means, and it was not the fiet time. My dad is an elderly person, he was fine catchimg the next flight a few hours later but the manner, the treatment he received, the lack of respect and making him pay a big difference for their mistake. Now he lost his luggage in the process... What happened to following up with him, calling his name through the radio prompt so he was awaee of the situation, sending a staff member to get him obviously knowing the passenger list...just a joke.

We recently booked an anniversary trip to Casablanca, with the second leg of our journey, costing approximately £900, booked directly through Royal Air Maroc. Unfortunately, both the outbound and return flights were severely disrupted due to last-minute cancellations. For the outbound journey, we were informed on very short notice that our flight would be redirected to a different airport, requiring us to travel to Heathrow instead of our original departure airport. This caused significant inconvenience. The situation worsened on our return flight during our anniversary in Casablanca. At extremely short notice, Royal Air Maroc cancelled our flight to Gatwick and rerouted us to Heathrow, leaving us no possibility of catching our connecting flights. Despite repeated attempts to seek assistance from Royal Air Maroc staff and customer service at Casablanca Airport, the experience was deeply disappointing. The staff were unhelpful, dismissive, and provided poor customer service. As a result of the return flight cancellation and rescheduling, we missed our connecting flights back home and were forced to purchase two expensive last minute British Airways tickets at our own expense to complete our journey.
Very dirty plane, I witnessed cockroaches Pleasee see pictures below. Fist class travelers have no first class bus. Bus was very hot and jamed.

A Nightmare with Air Maroc: Lost Luggage and Broken Promises I am writing this review to express my extreme disappointment and frustration with Air Maroc's handling of my lost luggage. In November 2023, my luggage was misplaced during a flight, and despite initial promises of a refund, the airline has since become unresponsive and unreliable. After numerous follow-up emails and phone calls, Air Maroc has completely ignored my inquiries. The promised refund has yet to be processed, leaving me in a state of uncertainty and financial distress. I urge potential travelers to exercise extreme caution when booking with Air Maroc. The airline's lack of customer service and disregard for its passengers is unacceptable. It is disheartening to experience such poor treatment from a major airline. I hope this review serves as a warning to others and prompts Air Maroc to take immediate action to rectify this situation.
The screen for my seat didn't work! The flight attendants were not friendly. There was a two hour delay. The airport in Casablanca was hot, and the restrooms had many doors that didn't lock

Warning: If you are a woman traveling alone, do not travel on this airline. I was awakened by a drunk man on my flight who insisted on sitting in my row while there was plenty of room to sit elsewhere on the plane. I told him to leave me alone and he proceeded to sexually harass me for must of the flight. The cabin crew (of primarily men) did absolutely nothing to help me. I will never fly this airline again and am warning all women that you are at risk if you fly on this airline.

So I was going to morroco for a family vacation and stuff the check in process was pretty decent. But the delays were reasonable, The plane got delayed for 35 minutes and I have patience so it wasn’t that long for me. The flight was 7.5 hours the inflight entertainment was Super outdated. However the crew was very impatient, I was putting down my fork to grab my drink and the flight attended slammed the drink on my tray table. The food was classic morrocsn food, But that was my experience I’m not sure if it was just thst crew or the whole RAM crew but they were not friendly at all

As a loyal customer who has traveled with your airline for years, I was deeply disappointed by my recent experience on November 4th from Paris Orly to Marrakech. Despite being an early bird at the airport, I faced disrespectful treatment from your check-in staff regarding my dog’s carrier, which was compliant with your own guidelines. Instead of assistance, I encountered belittling behavior and insults, which is unacceptable for any customer. This experience marred what should have been a joyful journey.

My experience was really bad with Royal Air Maroc. There was a couple hours delay in Casablanca. They boarded us on a bus to go to the plane but it was idle for 30 minutes with no communication. The food was terrible. I missed my connecting flight from Miami to Tampa. There was no one from the airline at their counter. I slept at the airport and went to the counter at 5 am and no one was there. I felt the crew did not care for us and our comfort. They should have provided us with food, transportation and hotel but they did not.

An overall awful experience. 7 hour delay with no communication. It was an hour after scheduled departure before before the gate agents even bothered to come to the gate. They had no information on flight, they just came to give us a $15 food coupon and then they left. They finally showed up 6 hours after regular scheduled flight and said they hoped we would leave in an hour. When we finally left the business class service was horrible. Food inedible. Seats broken. No front door to plane so as business class customer you are last off the plane - not great when your 8 hour connection is now 15 minute connection or be stranded for three days. As expected my luggage never arrived and they would not allow an on-line or phone claim. No employees at airport at 1:00 am arrival and next flight at IAD was three days in that is when employees return. Do you self a big favor and do not fly this airline

I have flown thousands of times, and this airline, without a doubt is the WORST I have ever encountered. Every single flight (round trip with layovers) was delayed. We spent over 36 hours trying to get home for what should have been a 3 hour trip. I would say they're customer service is horrible, but to honest, its non-existent as there are no employees to be found. I would recommend any other mode of transportation, such as a horse and buggy, before recommending this airline.

Do not use this airline! Lost bags, rude crew, no service to find lost bags, delays & no communication from crew or pilot about our delays . They do not update you- they expect you to call them daily. They are t helpful about what items you can purchase. The claim they have no faculty to call you! It’s the most awful lack of service I’ve ever experienced. Would never use again.

This airline is as bad as all the reviews say. Lack of communication on why our flight was delayed, flight attendant lied to us about our connecting flight holding for all of the passengers who were connecting and the plane itself was dirty and gross

Morocco is becoming the jewel of Africa and it was amazing to see the modern life of Rabat and Casablanca. And yet when it came to Royal Air Maroc, unfortunately there was nothing Royal or modern about it. While the people of Morocco are extremely friendly, the staff on this flight were sullen and lost. The business class section is old. The movies are all 10-15 years old and the seats next to each other are at different heights. But the crown jewel of the trip was the crazy meal served to us for a $4k ticket on a 9 hour flight. The meal had three ingredients- boiled potatoes, boiled lentils and boiled rice. No appetizers or dessert. It could be sheer incompetence. Or sheer corruption where the caterer gets to substitute boiled food in lieu of business class meals

HEATHROW TO LAGOS At thr stopover in Casablanca, the first taste of horror met us while unboarded and waiting for wheelchairs to connect the flight to final destination. I almost missed the fliga passenger missedhus connection flight (an elderly sickly gentleman and a lady who had to shed tears as she shouted loud for attentio HYGIENE The filthiest, most unkempt aircraft I ever boarded in my entire life, and I am a frequent flyer around the world on other airlines. Upon getting on board, you could smell and see filth all over and you had to practically use your hands to remove debris before sitting down. The passenger front pockets were an eyesore for each passenger. Only one toilet worked after a couple on hours - out of two dedicated to economy seats passengers on board this big aircraft. CUSTOMER SERVICE This was not bad, however, it was below standard. PASSENGER SAFETY AND SECURITY Non-existent as security belts were not checked even after announcements were made. FOOD Bland, tiny portions. The meal from Lagos to Casablanca was an apology. PRE-BOARDING This remains one never before now experienced. No word justifies the threats and verbal abuse permitted on passengers at the MMA boarding process points. Not a single RAM representative available for supervision to expected RAM standards. ARRIVAL HEATHROW One of three luggage missen. This was delivered three days later. No explanation, no apology. ENTERTAINMENT Quite another level of disappointment. The to and from Lagos legs were the most horrific.

To start - I had a standard experience of checking in for this flight and the customer service on the flight itself was good. However, I had the most horrendous experience of a lost bag I have ever experienced - in summary: never check in a bag with royal air maroc! I cannot stress how awful this experience was. I have detailed my specific experience below. When I arrived at Casablanca airport my bag I waited for my bag but it never arrived. I then went to the lost bag desk and after waiting for 30 minutes they eventually told me that lots of the bags on my flight hadn't arrived (prior to that only Arabic/Spanish was spoken so I didn't know what was going on - I speak English/French). I eventually was able to talk to a customer service representative who told me they didn't know where my bag was and that I could have it redirected to my next destination, Fes, which I agreed to. They offered no compensation for the loss of the bag, no way to track where it was and only provided a reference number for my bag and a number to call (which was challenging as I didn't have a sim!). Twice a day on my 7 day trip I would call for updates on the location of my bag and requested that they not re-route my bag to Fes but keep it in Casablanca (i.e. requested 10+ times). During these calls the staff gave me conflicting information on whether they had located my bag and where it was being sent - they all confirmed it would stay in Casablanca and not be sent to Fes and could not tell me whether it had arrived in Morocco or not. They provided no online way to track my bag or provide any written evidence that they had lost it - they refused to even send me any emails. The staff on the phone were rude, impatient and incompetent. At the end of my trip I was then informed that it had been sent to Fes despite my twice daily calls and assurances it would be kept in Casablanca. This was absolutely infuriating. This was after I went to Casablanca airport (and through three security checks and hours lost) to see if my bag was there. My bag was then re-routed to London and they refused to send it directly to my house but only to the airport. The bag desk also told me that I would have to ask Royal Air Maroc staff to receive compensation - I then went to the RAM staff at the airport and they were extremely dismissive of me, told me to go to other people, refused to help and sent me round in circles. I am a young female solo traveller and this was an extremely unpleasant and unnecessary experience. I eventually got my bag back weeks later and the London airport staff who were competent and helpful arranged to send it to my house for free. I have still not received any written evidence from RAM that they lost my bag, nor any compensation, nor an apology. I managed okay without my belongings but as my contact solution and backup contact lenses were in my checked-in bag I had to bin my monthly contact lenses (as stores were shut) and wear old prescription glasses for the entirety of my trip. I managed but not being able to see clearly was far from ideal. In summary: The experience of trying to get my lost bag back was 1000 times worse than spending the week without my belongings. Never check in a bag with Royal Air Maroc and better yet go with a different carrier entirely.

Our return flight to Brussels was just amazing. Clean toilet just for businesstraveller. Nice staff and worldclass service for a 3 and a half hour flight. You can‘t offer more. Compared to other airlines the offer and quality of food was gorgeous. Service 5star . Thanks to charming staff.Even the trolley they use to serve was well decorated.Definitely can recommend

I have traveled all my life all over the world but never had such a bad experience! My advise is pay more money for your ticket without hesitating one second. They lost my suitcases. It was almost impossible to make a claim in Miami and anyway was useless (an employee explain to me that they do not even use the barcodes of the suitcases at the connection in Casablanca and that 's why the "world tracer" is useless.) I sent them all the paperwork they were asking since my luggage were "lost" at the connection in Casablanca mid August. 2 months later I still have not received an email or anything from them. At the phone they only say that it is in process. HORRIBLE SERVICE!
Septembre 29/24 Sunday it was my worst day from Casablanca to Washington Dulles airport delayed over 5 hours is not only first time to many times is delayed without apology or claim

How did this lot get 2.5 stars? I've caught 600 odd flights and this was the worst with the most unhelpful staff of all of them. We were flying from Marrakech to Lisbon with a 25 hour stopover in Casablanca. THEY KEPT OUR BAGS! We were never asked at check-in whether we wanted our bags booked through and considering it was a 25 HOUR stopover, I didn't think to ask. When they didn't come off at Casa, we could see them through the tarmac glass. They said the only way they would give them to us was if we cancelled our flight completely (losing all money) and rebooked and paid for another. Our bags sat there for 25 hours with anyone from the airport having access to them for 25 hours. That stuff's scary!

Horrible service. my flight was about 11 hours delayed. No apology email was sent from the airline. where meals were served, started eating a piece of bread and the bread tasted moldy. On the return flight, my seat was dirty, read and brown spots on tray and the entertainment video system was not working. i sent them 2 complaint emails and they never responded.

AVOID AIR MAROC at all costs. I have never written a review before but this airline is beyond the pale. After getting in line to board my flight from JFK to Liberia, all passengers were asked to return to the gate as the aircraft was experiencing "technical issues". Four hours later, with infrequent updating on flight status, flight was cancelled. Forced to take another flight, my bags were never delivered to my final destination on their next available flight, No one in Air Maroc seems to know where my suitcases are. Call center is TOTALLY unhelpful. In order to request a refund on line for my ticket I was instructed to first call Air Maroc and officially cancel my flight which I did. Yet they keep sending me boarding updates to the flight I cancelled. I am waiting for further communication regarding my refund and suitcase status. I requested a refund from a previous flight that was cancelled in March; to date I have not received a refund. Numerous emails about looking into it but no refund. DO NOT EVER EVER fly on this airline. It distresses me to write this negative review, because I usually give airlines the benefit of the doubt, knowing that there are always occurrences out of their control. This was my first time flying with and hearing about Royal Air Maroc. My trip included one flight from Milan to Casablanca then another from Casablanca to DC. Pros: a one-way ticket from Milan to Washington DC with two checked bags and two carry-ons for less than $400? Unbeatable. Anything longer than a 2.5 hour flight, they feed you a hot meal. So going hungry on these flights were not an issue. For long flights they provide a pillow and a rather comfortable blanket. The service was great! You get the chance to step foot in Morocco, super cool. Both flights were relatively on time. No problem with receiving our baggage. Cons: our first airplane was significantly outdated, as a somewhat nervous flyer this made things tremendously worse. It did not look updated since the early 2000s and looked a bit dystopian. For conveniences, it lacked outlets/USB ports, inflight entertainment, WiFi, and bendable headrests. Seats in front of us were damaged and looked to be falling apart, mine had paint stains on it? An uncomfortable experience, I couldn't wait to get off the plane. Our second plane was much more upgraded however still pretty unclean. Someone had left a piece of gum on my seat that was never cleaned. Our tray tables also were stained and not wiped down. The bathrooms were not well kept. This plane had the faux purple leather just as the older ones which just are not too comfortable for very long. The food was just alright. Nothing to remember. The crew didn't speak the best English both flights, so anytime an announcement was made it was difficult to understand. Which again, makes a sticky situation for a nervous flyer during turbulence! 😬 Next time I'd probably just save up more money to go with a more reputable airline. For 12 hours of travel, I'd rather have a comfortable, clean, and more trustworthy experience. But hey, you can't beat the price.

The worst service at the airport. I have never experienced such poor customer service ever in my life. Flight delayed resulting in missing our connection. In transit they advised us the flight has left as captain would not wait. Issued a new ticket for 24hrs later. No compensation no apology no hotel nothing. They simply refused to help or assist. The purpose of our travel was for a bereavement. When calling the customer service line after arriving back in the U.K. - they simply would not accept any form of complaint and requested the complaint must be logged at the airport first. Even explaining the airport staff were unhelpful and did not help me or provide any support or log of a complaint. The people working for this airline are shockingly rude. There is no customer service with this airline. I would strongly advise you not to fly with this airline company.

We wanted to spend a few nights in Marrakech after arriving in Casablanca, and opted to take a domestic flight on Royal Air Maroc and back to Casablanca. Big mistake. First off, the airline insists you show up for check-in some 3 hours before departure. Seeing the long lines of travelers trying to get into the terminal at RAK suggests that this is not an idle suggestion. What's more, both airports are confusing with many contradictory signs, so we wound up running back and forth between Terminal 1 and 2 in search for our gate. Then--get this--we had to once again stand in a very long line for passport control in Marrakech, even though we passed through customs and passport control in Casablanca the night before! On a domestic flight! Followed, thereafter, by another passport check! Bottom line: if you want to go from Casablanca to Marrakech, take the train! It takes only two and a half hours, which is a whole lot faster than trying to travel on a domestic flight.

Re: Flight #: AT 200 Royal Air Morocco's treatment of black and African passengers at the airport and on their airline has left a negative impression on the Morocco Arab culture, and overall perception of Morocco. Specifically, there seems to be a lack of respect towards African Americans and African passengers, with instances of rudeness and perceived racism towards African Americans and Africans. Perhaps it is the negative perception they have learned in school or perhaps is their upbringing, nevertheless, It is challenging to find non black or mixed individuals in Morocco or with Royal Air Morroco who are not of black or mixed descent that speak respectfully to African or African American passengers. Simple questions can lead to confrontations and yelling, which I believe is a reflection of the cultural attitudes in Morocco that is aligned with the airline's service. Their aggressive attitude and rude behavior creates an intense atmosphere during the entire flight. I liked Morocco before in the past - the lack of respect, rudeness and ridicule expression of racism toward passengers that are not white or Arab has turned me off from anything Morocco. There's no reason to dislike others and pick and choose between who to respect and who not to in such an advance world. Morocco as a country, Air Morocco and businesses need to educate themselves about the dangers and impact of racism climate. At the airport, I encountered six extremely rude women before finally finding one who was willing to assist me. Additionally, there was an incident where a man yelled at my disabled mother when she struggled to climb stairs, claiming it was not his responsibility. However, he was helpful to a young Arabic-speaking woman at the same time. It's disappointing, disheartening and overall disrespectful. I have been to France so many times and I never experienced the level of disrespect, hostility, hate and racism towards black people like it common in Morocco. I even travelled to the villages in France and the people there are not as disrespectful. I understand that Morocco is a 3rd world, but the people should do better.

As a frequent traveler, I rely on airlines to provide a safe and comfortable journey, and unfortunately, my experience with Royal Air Maroc fell short of expectations. This review outlines the issues encountered during my recent flight, the second leg of my journey from New York to Istanbul. Arriving at CMN, a small airport, I anticipated a smooth transfer to my connecting flight. However, the use of a shuttle runner to board the plane on the tarmac presented an unexpected discomfort. The shuttle bus lacked adequate ventilation, subjecting passengers to stifling conditions with closed doors and no communication or driver for five minutes. This neglect of passenger comfort also posed a significant safety risk, which was deeply concerning. Upon boarding the plane, the discomfort escalated as the interior was exceptionally warm. The lack of adequate cooling led to distress among passengers, with reports of individuals becoming ill. Furthermore, the malfunction of approximately 85% of the in-flight entertainment systems was an additional disappointment. Requests for assistance regarding the non-functioning televisions were met with indifference from the attendants, who displayed a callous disregard for passenger experience. Adding insult to injury, the inoperative television screen installed upside down in my seat exemplified the lack of attention to detail. Although the flight eventually cooled down after takeoff, the initial discomfort and the prevalent technical issues tainted the overall experience. Despite these setbacks, the flight concluded without incident, and the plane landed safely at the destination. However, the singular food service providing a full meal, without any additional drinks or snacks, left much to be desired, especially in light of the other deficiencies experienced during the journey. In conclusion, my experience with Royal Air Maroc's CMN shuttle runner and in-flight service fell short of basic expectations. The lack of effective communication, discomfort, technical malfunctions, indifferent staff attitude, and limited food service collectively marred what should have been a seamless and enjoyable journey. As a passenger, I hope that Royal Air Maroc addresses these issues to enhance the travel experience for future customers.

This was a trick for me to shut up and go seat down, once we arrived, I asked for my luggage just to find out that it has been removed and left in Casablanca with no tag on it. Up to this day my luggage has not been found. THIS AIRLINE NEEDS TO BE STOPPED, THEY HAVE NO SHAME AND DO NOT DESERVE TO HAVE CUSTOMER
It’s not the first time or second Fly Morocco Airline company. Same problems every year. They horrible. The getting worse and worse

I had trip with this airline really I don’t recommend you to fly with Royal air maroc food (disaster) and staff are not that professional not all of them and i ask them to give me cover because i feel so cold they told me we don’t have imagine 8h without cover So i don’t recommend it and i will never fly with them Thanks

My Grandma suffered a lot with the airline Royal Air Maroc. She has hypertension and is diabetic so she needed a wheel chair, but as soon as she landed at Casablanca, they took her wheel chair from her, claiming that other elders needed it more. Despite that fact that we have paid for her to have it the entire trip. Now she has inflation in her legs for which she needed to go to the hospital for. They were very mean, and tried to scam her saying that her passport was not valid. If not for one young lady’s help she’d probably be stranded. Never use this airline.

I have never written a review but this was so terrible I want people to know they should never ever use this airline. They left 40-50 stranded in Casablanca and all they had to do was hold the flight a few minutes. Never got us to our final destination. Had to fly to JFK, rent a car and drive to DC.

Please avoid this airline they are the worst airline ever, I flew from London to Lagos Nigeria on the 8 July 2024 only to discovered that my luggage is missing till today after 1 week my luggage has not been found I was told that they can't track it to know the location while another lady that we came back together was told that her luggage is in Rwanda. One whole week gone and I have not found my luggage I was only meant to spend a day in lagos because I was going for a business trip in Accra. I had to cancel my flight to accra been paying for hotel in lagos and accra hoping to see my luggage, all my medication and documentations were in the missing luggage. I'm planning of suing them when I get back to England. This is the worst Airline PLEASE BOYCOTT ROYAL AIR MOROC.

They change the flight from Malaga to Casablanca and didn't change the connect flight to Washington DC so in that process we lost our connect flight which cost us big money and still could not get a penny from them after 7 month . Plus awful reservation system ( obviously!) the most inefficient customer service ever! Do not ever get even close to this airline , never. If you send anything to their serviceclient email you ll get a message saying do not send your meaasge to serviceclient@royalairmaroc.com Send it to serviceclient@royalairmaroc.com That is the same inbox , funny , right! They dont want you to contact them

This is the worst airline on Earth. You can imagine what we are currently going through. Flight time is significantly delayed and there has not been one customer service representative to address customers. This is a total failure. Never ever book this dead beat airline if you value your time and any events you had planned.

A friend recently posted on LinkedIn advising fliers never to fly Air Maroc. Today, July 13th, 2024, flying Air Maroc from Montreal through to Casablanca for a connecting flight, I had a 2 hour delay from Montreal. At Casablanca I missed the connecting flight, and was slammed with a replacement boarding pass for another flight scheduled for 24 hours! I wasn't the only passenger slammed with this. There were several others, many with little children and babies, and I cried as I saw them struggle to cope with the discomfort. No airline official to talk to us. Not any form of resources to ameliorate the pains. It is my second time on Air Maroc, and the first experience was equally unpleasant. I will keep all the boarding passes from this flight, and from today, I SHALL START A FULL FLEDGED CAMPAIGN AGAINST flying the airline. The airline is a disgrace to the hospitality that Arab nations are known for. I will go all out, on every form of social media, to show how badly the airline treats fliers. I have taken enough pictures of mothers and children sleeping on the floor and makeshift beddings from their luggage. The delay was not caused by weather conditions or natural events. It was totally the airlines problem! PLEASE NEVER CONSIDER FLYING AIR MAROC EVEN IF IT IT THE CHEAPEST THAT YOU COULD FIND! EVERYTHING HOSPITALITY ABOUT THE AIRLINE IS POOR! EXTREMELY POOR! MOROCCO SHOULD BE ASHAMED OF THIS ENTERPRISE! THE ARAB NATION NATIONS SHOULD BE ASHAMED OF IT! THE AIRLINE TREATS HUMANS LIKE STRAYED ANIMALS! PLEASE DO NOT PATRONIZE IT!
Terrible customer service. The people act as if they do not understand English when it comes to navigating the airport . Not willing to assist in any way

Worst customer service ever. We had a flight today paid 500£/person for it I'm allowed to 23kg checked in baggage, 10kg hand luggage + one personal item. The rude lady at check-in, in casablanca airport told us that the total of personal item and hand luggage has to be 10kg. Which I never heard of in my life. The problem was that the check-in luggage was only 18kg still she was insisting on reducing the hand luggage and the back pack which only had my laptop and tablet in it to 10kg. Otherwise I have to pay 40£ extra for it. She was so rude to the point she left us standing at the check-in counter and opened another one. I asked for a supervisor and then one off the check-in agents came and said she was the supervisor with no name tag and refused to give her name. I made her aware that I'm a pregnant lady who's really sick and you guys are stressing me out and making me do extra work for no reason as the total of my luggage is not over weight. She did not care about that and said if u pass out with the stress we will call an ambulance in a very rude way. I find this very disrespectful as a costumer who paid a lot of money for my flight and for someone who doesn't care about my welfare. This was my second bad experience with royal air maroc. I didn't complain before but this time they made me cry as no body deserve to be treated like this. I would not recommend this company to anybody.

1) we were allowed 4 suitcases. We had 3 . One bag was 2 lbs over . They asked us to move things to reduce the weight.2) #grumpyface : none of the front desk folks had a smile. They were possed to be at work 3) there about 5 hostess in the business class : they take turns to sleep on a 7 hour flight 4) this remind me of Indian Airlines in 2000 5) They have the World Cup in 2030 and they have and airline from 2000s 6 ) Bed are not lie flat 7)Food bring your own :8)Very old planes Easy to fix #service #customerservice #foodquality . They need to hire a new leader from a repair line like emirates or Singapore who can change the culture

This airline is not one I would ever use again. I only used them because their flight times were perfect for our vacation and they are a One World partner with American Airlines, but someone at AA should really evaluate that because this airline was pretty terrible. The plane was so dirty I can't even really find words to describe it. The food didn't seem terrible until we all had it exploding out of our body in the restrooms around Miami (sorry to multiple Miami businesses for what we did to your bathrooms as a result). No WiFi on a 9 hour flight and the screen in three of the four seats didn't work correctly. They also take any water you have, including what you buy in the airport and only give you a couple paper cups full of water for the entire flight. I wouldn't recommend this airline to anyone, including enemies!

HORRIBLE AIRLINE. Check in at Casa Blanca was terrible including fights at the baggage transfer lines. The plane was dirty, food was bad, we only had two glasses offered of water throughout the flight. Screens were not working in our area and so headsets did not work.

My experience with RAM was catastrophic, so much so that I vowed never to fly with them again, even if it cost me more. On a stopover in Casablanca (to Marrakech), the plane was delayed for over 6 hours. 6 hours of stress without any clear information being given, without water or snacks (we were stuck in a terminal with no shops or anything). After this incident (more than a year ago), the airline offered compensation but never made the transfer, despite hundreds of written messages by e-mail, twitter and telephone (a paying service). No doubt they hope their aggrieved customers will forget... This is a company to flee, unreliable and with an incompetent after-sales service.

Worst airline ever. Please please save yourself headache, money and time and DO NOT book this airline. The customer service is AWFUL on the phone. They shout at you, talk over you, tell you you're lying. They're VERY BAD. The airport staff have NO idea. It's totally useless. Please don't book this airline.

A good trip overall. The New York to Casablanca flight was during the day with a two hour layover in Casablanca before the flight to Monrovia. Both flights were full. The 3.5 hour flight from Casablanca to Monrovia left at 1:30 a.m., and they served a full meal. Both flights were full and the service was fine. The return flights were also ok. The crew on the Monrovia to Casablanca leg were a little less organized and seemed to have a harder team dealing with passengers on the full flight. A cold breakfast on that leg, but more than you get on many US airlines on international flights.

Do not use this airline. They took our bags, cancelled our flight and never cared to contact us regarding any of the incidents. Took over a week to get two of our bags back after over a dozen calls. No idea where the rest of them are. Everyone over the phone promises refunds and that the bags will be returned but no one ever follows through. We’ve been told multiple times someone will contact us but no ever one does. They are just trying to get you off the phone. Not sure how they can do business in the US.

Three other reviews from the same flight I was on here. We got diverted to Boston when our destination was IAD (Washington DC). Originally had to wait for a 3 hour delay in Casablanca. After take off, we’re then forced to exit the plane in Boston. The airline never gave us any method of getting to our destination. Had to miss a day of work and pay several hundred dollars in same day flight tickets to make it home. Still haven’t heard from RAM. Avoid. Avoid. Avoid.

Horrendous Experience. They lost my suitcase, which I guess happens, but I couldn't get in touch with anyone from their side to get any help. In addition, flight was delayed for an hour, then once we boarded, they kept us on the ground for 2 more hours, so that passenger from another delayed flight could catch this flight. In addition, Casablanca airport is the most stressful airport I have ever experienced. Airport staff trying to get you to tip them left and right, and Wi-Fi barely working. I will definitely be avoiding this airline at all costs.

America. Avoid this airline. Read the reviews. It's all true. Cancelled flights....they intentionally force American travelers to spend 24 hours in a garbage dump of a city, Casablanca. Stolen Luggage...yes, the staff will STEAL your luggage at the airport: you are NOT getting your luggage back! It's being sold to another tourist in a crime infested Medina. Rude, incompetent, and lazy staff across the board. A GIANT TOILET! In fact, I will NEVER travel through Morroco again, for any reason. Also, file a dispute with your VISA/Mastercard. Get the charges reversed.
Without a doubt, the worst customer service of any international airline. Theie service reps are arrogant, short tempered and very rude. Never fly this horrible airline.

This was the complaint that I sent to Royal Air Maroc Booking Reference: RA7MGF Flight Number: QR4567 On 29th March 2024 I booked my flight to Casablanca, Morocco as well as one from Casablanca to the UK. At the end of April, my husband visited the Royal Air Maroc office in Doha, Qatar, where we are residents. He went in to try and organise our seats as we were flying for the first time with our 10 month old son and wanted the bassinet seating. He was told that because we had booked early, we could not reserve seats. However, they were able to change mine and the baby’s seat from Morocco to the UK, my husband would be charged so we just stuck with my seat and the baby’s. They then told us that because the flight was Qatar Airways, we would need to reserve our seats through them; we tried and they told us it was a Royal Air Maroc flight and so it was up to them. We were told to just wait until we get to the airport and make the request then. On 14th June (the weekend before Eid) we had a flight 01.35 so because we knew it would be busy, we arrived at the terminal for 9pm. There was already a queue for the Royal Air Maroc check in, some people had queued for an hour already!. When check-in finally opened, the men ‘organising’ the queuing system had not taken any note of who had been queuing the longest and so they just let people go in any order. As you can imagine, arguments and fights broke out. By the time we got to check in, we were told that there were no bassinet seats left; what a surprise! Check in took so long that we had less than an hour to go to use the facilities, see duty free and then get ready for boarding. We were told to be ready for boarding at 12.35am! We got there early and the queue was huge. There was nowhere to sit and it was chaos. There was no way that the flight was leaving at 01.35am. Usually, with most other airlines, when boarding is ready, business class goes first, then families and then in order of the plane from back to front. Of course, this did not happen with Royal Air Maroc, it was a free for all. There were elderly people, pregnant women, families with small babies all being knocked about by those who just wanted to get on the flight. We stayed and waited, not wanting our child to have to sit on a stuffy plane for any longer than need be. Quite honestly, it was a disgrace. When we finally saw the back of the queue, one of the ladies checking boarding passes moved us forward, not that it made much difference because the man getting everyone on the bus was horrendous. He was trying to put so many people on the bus that he asked us to take a baby out of the pushchair we had whilst he was fast asleep just so he could squeeze us on the bus. This meant that we had a grumpy baby, standing room only, and when the bus moved, we were being thrown all over the bus so my husband was struggling to hold our 10kgs son when we could have just waited for the next bus! On the flight, our luggage just got scattered across the plane. We had a window seat and a middle seat, luckily the person in the aisle seat was very understanding. As our son wanted to sleep, I had to lay him on my lap and the food tray in front of me for him to get any comfort. I missed the meal and there was no consideration for this later. No one checked in and asked if I needed anything. My husband shared his food with me. Because we were busy with our son, we missed the point where they collected the food trays in, so we called for help to have it removed so we could change our son’s nappy and then we could rest. It took 30mins for someone to come and help us! The staff could be seen chatting and having a drink but not one came out until they were done. So my son was screaming to be changed much to the annoyance of passengers I’m sure all because not one cabin crew member could be bothered to come and check on us. Please note that we weren’t the only ones waiting. By the time the cabin crew finished, there were 6 lights on just in our section! Finally, why don’t you have female cabin crew in all areas? Is it appropriate to only have males? Overall, if I had to choose next time, I would go with the Qatar Airways flight in the morning. I just hope that more passengers complain because the situations above are avoidable with some common sense and basic organisation skills.

Despite arriving 3 hrs before departure and standing in queue with only 5 passengers ahead of me the luggage checkin too so long by the time we reached the desk we were told it was too late - this despite one woman ( the supervisor apparently) walking up and down telling passengers for Brussels to move to the front but nothing about London (our destination) - when I asked her she replied - we are not obliged to alert you (????!!!!!) literally each person checking in ahead of us took 20 mins minimum to have a tag put on their bag and for some reason this had to be done manually each time by the employee who, each time, left her desk to fetch said tickets from another location. She then took each persons passport to someone else to be double checked - why? Beginning to wonder if she even worked there or if it was some kind of candid camera set up - whole thing a joke - had to pay for another flight, extra accommodation etc - total nightmare start to finish - customer service where I rebooked couldn’t have cared less - mentioned we had arrived at airport on time I was told - we will check cctv cameras!!! Security just as bad - customs guy stopping all attractive young girls asking them questions waving everyone else through, security police leaving you standing 5-10 mins to be waived through the scanner while they text their mates, passport control like the baggage check in - didn’t seem to have a clue how to use their computers, running about with peoples passports to double check them with other colleagues standing around behind them - honestly crazy, frustrating and staff downright rude and incompetent - so sad that such a beautiful country and wonderful people so badly let down by their airport and airport staff - puts me off going back sadly.

What first appears a discount airline fare must be reconsidered in terms of comfort, reliability and communication. It fails woefully on each count. A smaller company with little reserves- one maintenance problem means one day delay, provided accommodations are simply awful, and communication nonexistent. Staff friendly but overwhelmed, hours waiting on airport tarmac/in lounge/in parking lot only to be canceled, thus making connections impossible. Old plane, poorly maintained outside and in, no drinks nor food, short staffed- what’s not to like? Old adage: pay more, get more.

Flying with Royal Air Marco was an absolute nightmare. There was a 3.5 hour delay with no communication. After finally getting on the plane the journey was fine until they told us 30 minutes out from our destination that they were no longer landing there and instead re-routing to Boston. They held us on the plane for 2 hours with no outlets and zero service. They provided 0 information. All they said was that information was coming. Someone would come. They never did. Boston Logan doesn’t even have a staff for Royal Air Morac. All they did was tell us stay and wait. Because we did as they advised, we all got stuck after hours at the airport completely stranded to find our own accommodations and travel plans. I spend over $500 trying to get a last minute hotel and renting a car. The drive home was 10 hours. Still , 4 days later and we’ve heard nothing. The lack of communication is the worst part. If they were upfront that they didn’t know what was going on and that they couldn’t help us, everyone could’ve helped themselves faster. They left pregnant women, small children, and everyone stranded at midnight and offered no help or refunds. It was a terrible experience and they should be embarrassed by how poorly they handled everything.

This was a bad ending to an otherwise wonderful trip. Our flight home from a Mediterranean Cruise was supposed to be from Barcelona to Casablanca, with an hour layover, then on to our final destination of Dulles. The first flight was fine. The connecting flight from Casablanca took off 3 1/2 hours late. There was very little communications on why we were late and what was going on. Once in the air, we were notified the plane was being diverted to Boston. When we landed, we were told there was technical issues and we had to remain on the plane. After more than an hour, they let us off and told us to retrieve our luggage. Once off the plane, there was no one at the airport to provide us with any information. We were not given food or hotel vouchers. We were not given alternatives to getting to our final destination. After a few hours, the airport supplied cots for us to sleep on. The crew came through about 3 hours after we arrived. We were asking questions of the crew and not one of them opened their mouth to tell us what was going on. They simply walked away. We finally made arrangements to get home by train. We received 3 emails stating we would get an update by 15:00, then 16:00 and finally 18:30. Needless to say, no update every came. We still don't know what happened. Hopefully everyone finally made it home. The airline should be ashamed of themselves for how they treated paying customers! Communication is always the best practice.

On June 23rd, around 11:00 PM EST. The right engine of Boeing 787 Royal Air Maroc AT218 failed 2 hours from any land. We emergency landed at Boston where no staff were to be found. We were left stranded with no representatives, no info on flight, no vouchers for hotels. People were missing luggage, people didn't speak english, and people were scared. 261 people were left stranded in one of the most expensive cities in the world 10 hours driving distance from our intended destination with all flights from other airlines being 500$ or more for a flight the next day. 27 hours later and Royal Air Maroc has yet to even acknowledge what happened. The flight that was supposed to be after our flight from Dulles to Casablanca has now been delayed over 24 hours with Royal Air Maroc also refusing to offer hotels, vouchers, and explanations to those passengers. They were also aware the plane was having engine issues while we were waiting in Casablanca as they delayed our flight by 4 hours due to "technical issues" and decided to fly it anyway. We landed with 1 working engine to emergency vehicles. On my flight from Dulles To Casablana, had no issues. On way back from Casablanca to Dulles, worst day of my life. Avoid them. Fly to europe for a day and take a european airline the next. As they care about their passengers.

Royal Air Maroc, you still owe me $341.91! RAM completely destroyed my luggage and STILL hasn't reimbursed me after months of calling and emailing. The absolute worst customer service from any company I have ever experienced. Avoid at all costs. Just awful.

Yesterday I meet the most unfriendly and rude stewarders in my all flights in my life . I left my phone on the airplan. My next fly was after hour and a half I had time to catch the next plane to Amsterdam. I had to run from one douane to another and inquire. In the end they said that my phone would be given to me at the geat by the security. I went to the geat but they didn't know enything about it ? they send me to the airplan and they said security will give it on me there. I went to the airplan I wanted to tell her my story, but she didn't even let me finish, what i was saying , she said that I had to go to teak my seat! as we know in these cases if you refuse they can create all kinds of problems for you.So I had to leave my phone behaind. Everybody know how important a mobile phone can be for someone. They had find my telephon but she wouldent listen to me!! I Would never fly with this airline again or recommend this to no one.

We were on a short flight from Barcelona to Casablanca. Check-in was slow but nice and friendly (the agent managed to move my wife and I to sit together despite us not being able to get seats together through the online check-in). Boarding was slow. The flight itself was smooth enough. They gave a small snack of tuna sandwich, cupcake, yogurt, and a requested beer (absolutely horrendous Flag Speiciale). Sadly, things turned from mediocre to much worse upon arrival in Casablanca, when I found my luggage had been badly damaged. After waiting in line at a "customer service" baggage counter for more than 30 minutes (with staff milling around doing nothing and uncaring about customers), when I finally reached the counter, they sent me to a second baggage service counter on the other side of the terminal. More waiting until finally an agent quickly provided me with a claim number and said I needed file a claim online. I've done so, but am yet to receive a response other than an acknolwedgment of my request for reimbursement for the damage.

The experience was not good at all . The flight departure was delayed from Dubai 1 hr and few minutes, it supposed to be at 14:55 the thing is that we had to go to Casablanca for transit to reach Marrakesh … the team already know about the delay which will lead us to miss the next flight but they said it is ok the next flight will wait for you , when we reached the airport to take the flight to Marrakesh which was at 21:50 the door was closed nd nobody was waiting for us as it was mentioned before , we were almost 9 people who didnt attend the plan . We asked for the nearest flight nd they gave us boarding pass with a flight at 22:50 - unfortunately we had to wait until 1:10 AM . We reached the airport at 2:25 AM tired nd no energey . They supposed to refund us if they really care about their passengers or else they should provide good service as we are paying money not rocks …

Upgrading to business class at Royal Air Maroc counter in Morocco. Asked the counter lady to confirm if business class was a "regular" one with seats that recline to a bed. She confirmed - of course! Turned out - regular seats with no TV screen. What a SCAM. DON'T get a business class upgrade - you'll just throw away your money

The worst airline ever!! This was my first time flying this airline. I am very disappointed in the quality of the aircraft and the service by the flight attendants. The negative vibes started with a visit to the Royal Air Maroc lounge in Casablanca. I hesitate to call it lounge. It was extremely crowded , only one shower and very hard to find. I ended up using a priority pass lounge, which was much nicer. At the gate in Casablanca, total disorganization by the ground crew. First, we were told gate 9 and as we were lining up, the gate was switched to 8, with no one answering any questions and being quite rude. Plus, we started boarding at 10:25 when we were supposed to depart at 10:55. Then I get to my seat, 3A in business. There was a tiny little pillow on my seat, and no blanket. Also, there was a big gap between the seat back and the seat bottom. which made it very uncomfortable for seven hour flight I have flown with numerous airlines and the flight attendants always come around to offer water or drinks during the flight. Once food service was over, they never came around offering drinks. The seatbelt came on; on other airlines, the flight attendants always come around to check to make sure the passengers have put on their seatbelts. On this flight noone came around to check. Also, the lavatory was a mess. Usually the flight attendants check and tidy the bathroom. This flight definitely did not feel like a business class experience. I sent an email to Royal Air Maroc and have not received a response.

Racist, irresponsible, scam, and rude Royal Air Maroc has been the worst airline I have traveled with. I hope many people read this message and don't buy tickets to fly with them. I flew from Miami to Madrid with a connection in Casablanca and my suitcases never arrived, I made a claim and the people in charge and costumer service always have the same rude answer: “we do not have an update for the airport” “what is your problem, it is just a month you have to wait” and they even mistreated me just because of my accent; I could hear people laughing in the background. I have wrote many emails, I have made a claim for the refund or compensation and I have not received any answer. They are irresponsible, they lose your suitcases, they are a disaster, they treat you rudely and hang up the phone, They laugh at your desperation and your accent. It's quite sad to know that these are the values upheld by the company. I am going to take this as far as I can. It's unacceptable. I will post it on all social media platforms and on my professional profile. These are not the values of the United States of America. THE WORST AIRLINE IN THE WORLD EVER ….

I have used this airline before but trust me, the 1st time I had been on an aeroplane where I had to clean the seat before sitting and the last is Royal Air Maroc!!! I have never felt so humiliated in all my life when ignored on this flight from London Gatwick to Banjul. I am vegetarian and just wanted to know if I could receive another fruit juice instead of fruits as I am allergic to dairy and did not wish to cause drama on the flight, so I strictly stayed away from dairy. After being late by an hour the baggage claims decided NOT to load all customers' luggage onto the plane so though I was first in the queue at the airport and hours early, I was more than disappointed to realise when landing in Banjul that my luggage with mostly stuff for charity had been left in Morocco! on purpose to make up for the lost time and being late. Then after a month and halfway through my trip, going to the Maroc office in Banjul only to find out and realise that though receptionists had been working there for 15 years they had no training in customer service and I had to teach them how to do their jobs and take them seriously! I advise you now if you are reading this to NEVER trust this airline as the seats on the plane are dirty, worn and tattered and there is NOTHING royal about this airline. I will never waste time, energy or money again on them and when they mess with the wrong customer they will finally be sued and go out of business. Ase. One of the largest disasters in my life and this has never happened in all my travelling days before! I, family and friends are horrified at this airline!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
It wasn't the worst flight ever nor the best. The airline is average with below average entertainment and food choices. It was on time, so there is a positive aspect.

TERRIBLE. Dirty, very dirty. Food: just fish (same odoro than the plan) Worst of all: they have LOST OUR LUGGAGE. No one signed up a claim, we just filled a form but couldn’t get a claim/ref number to follow up, just take a pocture. Till ow, no one called/email us, no one answers any phone, mails get auto-answers.

This is the WORST airline I have EVER flown. Please do yourself a favor and do not fly with this airline. Each flight we had was delayed, almost missed our second connecting flight, had the worst customer service, they lost our 2 bags, they don’t have the technology to “track” your bag like other airlines do and customer service is an absolute JOKE - completely makes you go through hoops. They really do nothing for their customers. I know this is a cheaper way to fly around but you’re better off booking Spirit than this fools

Absolute nightmare of an experience. They lost all of our luggage, and offered no help to try and resolve the issue. Staff was rude and changed my wife’s seat as we were boarding the plane without explanation. (We were in row 10, she got moved to row 33) we flew 10 hours on 2 opposite ends of the plane. On the return flight one flight attendant was smoking a vape in the back of the plane. The list of issues goes on but the biggest thing is the lost luggage. I have never had an airline not be able to give any help with luggage that they lost. Do not fly this airline, you will regret it!

Absolutely horrific experience. Flight from DC to Lisbon, with a connection in Casablanca, went as scheduled. The issue was that ~8 hour overnight flight had no AC and was sweltering hot with half the entertainment screens on the flight non functional. The intiaip flight from Barcelona back to the US was delayed 3 hours after boarding. While sitting in the aircraft for those 3 hours, passengers were never given any updates aside from a very vague “issues with paperwork” and the continuous “we are taking off shortly” every 10-20 minutes. Connecting flights with the same airline were never delayed and replacement flights were scheduled for 48 hours later. After waiting at the Royal Air Maroc counter for an additional 3 hours (many people gave up an accepted the 48 hour alternate flight), flights back to Lisbon and then to the US (with a different airline) were booked for those remaining. We were offered overnight stay in a poor quality hotel outside of Casablanca during the delay. I will absolutely never even consider flying with Royal Air Maroc again as this is the worst service, communication, and overall flying experience I’ve ever been subjected to, in what is already a universally awful industry in air travel. I would pay several hundreds more for a run of the mill, average airline. This one is truly bottom of the barrel.

Please please please don’t use this airline. First, there is always a problem with timing , always late. Customer service inside the plane is horrible, bathrooms don’t get cleaned for 8 hours flight , food is horrible . 90% they will loose your baggage and won’t replace it . There is non communication. Pay an extra $100 and use a better airline . I flew with them for 20 years from USA and every time I say this is the last one . They even didn’t compensate us for lost baggage.

Worst airline on the planet. Every employee you meet is somehow dumber than the next and rude. Airline lost my bag and didn’t even pretend to look for it while I was on a foreign country with no belongings. 8 nights, zero progress. Do not fly. It would be easier to swim across the Atlantic. Royal Air Maroc did not bring my hold back to Paris on 8 March, I raised the loss in the airport on 8 March. I raised a complaint on 11 March online with RAM.
